sub_task_list.user_prompt: There’s been some executive noise lately about approvals stalling for avoidable access issues, so treat this as one of those cross-team recoveries that needs to look orderly even if the surrounding chatter is not. By the time everything is wrapped up, the internal HR knowledge article numbered 5 should remain titled “IT Security Best Practices,” be visible only inside the company, stay published under owner 39, and have added guidance consisting of a reusable recovery checklist for HR portal account access, ownership, and same-day escalation steps. Before that handoff reaches the knowledge-base side, the collaboration operations specialist should have been brought in by the IT desk layer so that, from olivia.chen@techcorp.com, a plain-text note goes to helen.zhou@techcorp.com, ivan.park@techcorp.com, and priya.nair@techcorp.com with the subject “HRC0000767 recovery plan” and the message that HRC0000767 needs same-day recovery for HR portal account access and asks them to bring the current blocker, owner, and next checkpoint; in the same stretch, a meeting should be placed on alice-primary for 2026-04-22 from 14:30:00+08:00 until 15:00:00+08:00, as a recovery huddle reviewing blockers and owners for HR portal account access, including those same three attendees and sending updates to all. That collaboration step should only occur after the IT service engineer has been asked in, and that engineer should open a fresh incident for USER_022, coming in by email, under password-reset, with medium impact but maximum urgency and therefore high priority, routed to GROUP_002 and assigned to the same person, carrying the short line “HRC0000767 blocked by HR portal account access dependencies,” along with the explanation that HR escalated HRC0000767 because the employee still cannot access the HR portal account and approval is blocked, plus a note that it was opened from the HRC0000767 recovery workflow. Earlier still, on the HR side, case 57 — the one also numbered HRC0000767 — needs to be moved into work in progress, given to Helen Zhou, and annotated exactly with “HR portal account access blocked; cross-team recovery started”; immediately after that, a checklist-style child task under that same parent should be prepared to begin for her as well, using “Coordinate recovery for HR portal account access” as the short description and “Track access, document, and approval dependencies for HR portal account access.” as the work note, because the employee still cannot access the HR portal account and approval remains blocked.
